2606.07515.title: How reliable are LLMs when it comes to playing dice?
2606.07515.paper_summary: We investigate the probabilistic reasoning capabilities of large language models through a controlled benchmarking study on discrete probability problems. We constructed two datasets, respectively a set of standard exercises and a set of counterintuitive exercises, designed to trigger heuristic reasoning, and evaluated 8 state-of-the-art models, each tested with and without Chain-of-Thought prompting. Models achieve an average accuracy of 0.96 on standard problems but only 0.59 on counterintuitive ones. We further provide empirical evidence of token bias: performance drops by over 20% when canonical formulations are replaced by disguised variants. Embedding misleading suggestions in the prompt reduces performance by up to 34%, with no model proving immune. Taken together, the reported findings suggest that current LLMs are not yet genuine probabilistic reasoners, despite their success in advanced mathematical problems.
2606.07515.paper_summary_zh: 我們通過對離散概率問題的控制基準研究，調查大型語言模型的概率推理能力。<lb/>我們構建了兩個數據集，分別是一組標準練習和一組反直覺練習，旨在觸發啟發式推理，並評估了8個最先進的模型，每個模型在使用和不使用思維鏈提示下進行測試。<lb/>模型在標準問題上的平均準確率為0.96，但在反直覺問題上僅為0.59。<lb/>我們進一步提供了標記偏見的實證證據：當經典公式被偽裝變體替換時，性能下降超過20%。<lb/>在提示中嵌入誤導性建議會使性能下降高達34%，且沒有模型顯示出免疫力。<lb/>綜合來看，報告的發現表明，儘管目前的LLM在高級數學問題上取得了成功，但它們尚未成為真正的概率推理者。
2606.07515.author: Luca Avena et.al.
2606.07515.authors: Luca Avena, Gianmarco Bet, Bernardo Busoni
